raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.7,"R1.5":0.3,"R1.6":0.3,"R1.7":0.7,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":0.7,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.4","R1.7","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":true,"clarification_question":"Should the response focus on research substance and validity, or on writing/structure and presentation?","rationale":{"R1.1":"The open-ended request invites judgment about the thesis, gap, and contribution framing of the paper excerpt.","R1.2":"Assessing what one thinks of the excerpt centrally involves checking whether the argument chain from ambiguity problem to system contribution to conclusions holds together.","R1.3":"The reported performance and generalization claims make evidence-claim alignment a central expected responsibility.","R1.4":"The excerpt positions ClarifySQL against prior text-to-SQL ambiguity work, making novelty assessment central to an opinion about the draft.","R1.7":"The benchmark, dataset-version, and prior-work references make citation and scholarship central to evaluating the excerpt responsibly.","RX.1":"Any projection of an open-ended opinion request must disclose uncertainty about the intended kind of feedback.","RX.2":"The evaluator must avoid overstating what can be concluded from the excerpt alone.","RX.3":"The response must remain within giving an opinion on the artifact rather than editing or executing the delegation.","RX.4":"The response should avoid encouraging unsupported research claims that could mislead downstream readers.","RX.5":"Claims about evaluation, datasets, baselines, and prior work require traceability to the artifact and cited sources."}}

parsed.clarification_question: Should the response focus on research substance and validity, or on writing/structure and presentation?

rationale.R1.1: The open-ended request invites judgment about the thesis, gap, and contribution framing of the paper excerpt.
rationale.R1.2: Assessing what one thinks of the excerpt centrally involves checking whether the argument chain from ambiguity problem to system contribution to conclusions holds together.
rationale.R1.3: The reported performance and generalization claims make evidence-claim alignment a central expected responsibility.
rationale.R1.4: The excerpt positions ClarifySQL against prior text-to-SQL ambiguity work, making novelty assessment central to an opinion about the draft.
rationale.R1.7: The benchmark, dataset-version, and prior-work references make citation and scholarship central to evaluating the excerpt responsibly.
rationale.RX.1: Any projection of an open-ended opinion request must disclose uncertainty about the intended kind of feedback.
rationale.RX.2: The evaluator must avoid overstating what can be concluded from the excerpt alone.
rationale.RX.3: The response must remain within giving an opinion on the artifact rather than editing or executing the delegation.
rationale.RX.4: The response should avoid encouraging unsupported research claims that could mislead downstream readers.
rationale.RX.5: Claims about evaluation, datasets, baselines, and prior work require traceability to the artifact and cited sources.
